description: Git worktree flow manager — one-command worktree creation with tmux sessions, Docker isolation, GitHub PR/issue integration, and lifecycle hooks. Replaces 5-10 manual shell commands per context switch.
rules: Grove replaces manual git worktree + tmux + Docker orchestration. Use grove new/to/rm instead of git worktree add/remove + manual setup.
Shell integration is REQUIRED for directory switching: eval "$(grove install zsh)" in ~/.zshrc. Without it, grove prints cd: directives instead of changing directories.
grove new <name> creates worktree + git branch + tmux session + runs hooks (copy credentials, symlink node_modules, start Docker) — all in one command.
grove to <name> is an atomic context switch: changes directory, fires pre/post-switch hooks, starts/stops Docker, attaches tmux. Use --peek to switch without side effects.
grove fetch pr/N and grove fetch issue/N create worktrees directly from GitHub — no manual gh/git commands needed.
grove fork <name> creates a parallel worktree from current HEAD. Use --move-wip to transfer uncommitted changes, --copy-wip to duplicate them.
For parallel agents: grove up --isolated gives each agent its own Docker stack with auto port offsets. No port conflicts between agents.
Config is TOML: project .grove/config.toml overrides global ~/.config/grove/config.toml. Run grove config to see merged config.
Worktree names follow {project}-{name} pattern. grove ls shows short names, tmux sessions use full names.
grove test <worktree> runs tests in another worktree without switching — agents can test in parallel without interference.
AGENT WARNING: grove to can attach tmux sessions, taking over the terminal. Agents should set [tmux] mode = 'manual' or 'off', or use grove to --peek to skip tmux and hooks.

For external Docker mode with env_file = '.env.local': use mise (.mise.toml with [env] _.file = '.env.local') or direnv (.envrc with dotenv_if_exists .env.local) so manual docker compose commands see the env vars.
